content.problemStatement:
Chef likes tournament graphs, which are directed graphs where each unordered pair of vertices is directly connected by exactly one edge. A directed graph is strongly connected if for each pair of vertices (a,b)(a, b)(a,b), there is a path from the vertex aaa to the vertex bbb.

Consider a tournament graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). Chef takes a sequence of pairs of vertices (a1,b1),(a2,b2),‚Ä¶,(aM,bM)(a_1, b_1), (a_2, b_2), \ldots, (a_M, b_M)(a1‚Äč,b1‚Äč),(a2‚Äč,b2‚Äč),‚Ä¶,(aM‚Äč,bM‚Äč) and does the following for each iii from 111 to MMM in this order:


Flip the direction of the edge between vertices aia_iai‚Äč and bib_ibi‚Äč.
If the graph is strongly connected either before or after flipping this edge, declare GGG a bad tournament.
If GGG is never declared a bad tournament, Chef calls it a good tournament. Given the sequence of MMM edge flips, find a good tournament.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 4,0004,0004,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äč and bib_ibi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain N‚ąíiN-iN‚ąíi space-separated integers; for each valid jjj, the jjj-th of these integers should be 111 if there is an edge from the vertex iii to the vertex i+ji+ji+j or 000 if there is an edge from the vertex i+ji+ji+j to the vertex iii.

It can be proved that a good tournament always exists under the given constraints. If there are multiple solutions, you may print any one of them.
